The classification of G. binervis is consistent with the most recent updates to the botanical classification system.

intro: Gladiolus binervis, also known as the two-veined gladiolus or narrow-leaved gladiolus, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Iridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its health benefits, which include the reduction of inflammation, improvement of respiratory issues, and its antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Gladiolus binervis include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
